title: Second exemplar batch in a 2nd target language, reviewed
context: M1's definition of done requires a second exemplar batch reviewed across a second target language, demonstrating that the pipeline is repeatable and not limited to a single language pair. This task translates the exemplar source descriptions (from exemplar-001) or a new openly-licensed batch into a second target language using the glossary-002 language pair. A qualified bilingual reviewer for the new language must sign off before the batch is considered done.
objective: Translate the approved exemplar source descriptions into a 2nd target language (the language pair covered by glossary-002): apply glossary-002 throughout; preserve all proper nouns, dates, materials, and period terms per the glossary's preserve/transliterate conventions; preserve plain-ness in the target language; flag all uncertainty with UNCERTAIN tags; and obtain qualified bilingual reviewer sign-off for the new language pair recorded in review.yaml and the PR.
acceptanceCriteria: Each exemplar source description from exemplar-001 (or a new openly-licensed batch) is translated into the 2nd target language covered by glossary-002
Glossary-002 applied throughout: glossary terms used consistently, proper nouns handled per preserve/transliterate/do-not-translate conventions for the new language pair
Plain-ness preserved in the 2nd target language: translations are label-sized, clear, and jargon-free for a general visitor reading in the target language
All agent UNCERTAIN flags captured in review.yaml agentFlags and resolved before reviewer sign-off; grounding flags trigger claim review not translation of a potentially erroneous source claim

Qualified bilingual reviewer for the 2nd target language (native or near-native competence with heritage-translation experience) signs off in the PR and review.yaml; reviewer independent of translator, COI declared
Per-translation attribution/credit line and output license (source-compatible, never more permissive than source) recorded in attribution.txt for each translated description
resources: multilingual-museum-labels-glossary-002 (2nd language pair glossary — must exist)
output: objects/<objectId>/descriptions/<tgt2-lang>.md for each exemplar object; updated review.yaml and attribution.txt for each object
